images.alt: Selecting the Right Plastic for Your Next Molded Part
Thermoplastic and thermoset materials used for parts in injection molding

meta_description: Thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) is a flexible thermoplastic material that offers abrasion resistance, chemical resistance, impact strength, and overall toughness.

sections.heading: TPU Injection Molding
sections.text: TPU Injection Molding Molded TPU parts exhibit excellent abrasion resistance and flexibility Get a Quote

sections.heading: Molded TPU parts exhibit excellent abrasion resistance and flexibility
sections.text: Molded TPU parts exhibit excellent abrasion resistance and flexibility

sections.heading: TPU Material Properties
sections.text: TPU Material Properties Flexural Modulus Abrasion Resistance Tensile Stress Tear Strength Tensile Elongation (Break) Test Method ASTM D790 ASTM D1044 ASTM D412 ISO 34-1 ASTM D412 Texin 245D 73F: 9,990 psi 158F: 7,500 psi 70 mg 50% strain: 1,100 psi 100% strain: 1,310 psi 300% strain: 3,000 psi 700 lb/fin 500% Texin 983A -22F: 8,560 psi 73F: 3,900 psi 30 mg 100% strain: 798 psi 300% strain: 1,200 psi 500 lb/fin 670% Uses for Molded TPU Parts The mechanical properties of TPU make it a durable material and well suited for parts such as: Wheels Gears Hose Gaskets Footwear Tubing
